one_line_thesis: Multi-brand home compounder ($7.8B revenue, 65% e-commerce) with strong owner cash and buybacks; watch at ~$217 on 4.24% synthesis IRR below 15% hurdle.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Williams-Sonoma is a multi-brand home compounder: $7.81 billion of FY2025 revenue, $1.06 billion of free cash flow, and 46.2% gross margin on an asset-light, e-commerce-heavy model. Company comparable brand revenue grew 3.5% in FY2025 and 4.8% in Q1 FY2026; Williams Sonoma brand comp was +6.9% in FY2025 on kitchen strength.

At about $217.06 per share (2026-07-10), the Lawrence base case implies about 4.24% per year on normalized owner cash of $8.57 per share, below our 15% accumulate hurdle. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable: design-led private label, loyalty (The Key R...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Leadership / governance risk elevated. Williams-Sonoma (NYSE: WSM) CEO sells 35,000 shares under Rule 10b5-1 plan - Stock Titan; 43 executive sales ($66,075,331) in 120d
Q1 FY2026 earnings: net revenue +4.4%, company comparable brand revenue +4.8%, brand comp +1.0%; dividend raised 15% quarterly.
FY2025 results: net revenue $7.81B (+1.2%), company comp +3.5%, operating income $1.42B; quarterly dividend increased 15%.
FY2024 results: net revenue $7.71B; company comp +3.5%; continued store optimization and buybacks.
Continued retail store rationalization: 506 company-operated stores at FY2025 year-end versus 512 prior year.
FY2024 repurchased ~$807M of common stock; FY2025 repurchased ~$854M while paying $327M dividends.
Laura J. Alber continues as CEO and President; long-tenured operator-led capital allocation (DEF 14A 2026).
Launched GreenRow sustainable home brand; expanded emerging-brand portfolio alongside Rejuvenation and Mark and Graham.
Established Mark and Graham monogrammed lifestyle brand.
Acquired Rejuvenation lighting and hardware brand (founded 1977).
Launched West Elm contemporary furniture brand.
Acquired Pottery Barn (founded 1949); became multi-brand home platform.
Williams-Sonoma specialty cookware retailer founded in Sonoma, California.

industry.structure: Fragmented U.S. home furnishings market with department stores, pure-play e-commerce, mass merchants, and specialty retailers; WSM competes on design-led private label across multiple price points.
industry.share_shift: WSM gaining share in premium omnichannel home through Pottery Barn and Williams Sonoma kitchen while closing underperforming stores; e-commerce now 65% of net revenue.
industry.trend: Tariffs and freight costs pressure gross margin; furniture and kitchen categories outperform seasonal decor; loyalty (The Key Rewards) and B2B sales deepen repeat purchase.

Company comparable brand revenue grew 3.5% in FY2025 and 4.8% in Q1 FY2026; Williams Sonoma brand comp was +6.9% in FY2025 on kitchen stre
At **$217.06** per share, the base case implies **4.24%** per year on FY2025 owner cash of **$8.57** per share; Marvin proposes **watch**.

risk_claims.claim: Leadership / governance risk elevated. Williams-Sonoma (NYSE: WSM) CEO sells 35,000 shares under Rule 10b5-1 plan - Stock Titan; 43 executive sales ($66,075,331) in 120d
*Primary risk: Housing turnover and discretionary home spend weaken, pushing comparable brand revenue negative and compressing the 46% gross margin through tariffs, freight, and promotional depth.
Inversion: If furniture and kitchen comps stay positive through a housing lull (as Q1 FY2026 +4.8% suggests), the market may be correctly paying up for durability rather than mispricing cyclicality.
